market_summary: Mixed-to-fragile regime: SPY -3.42% 1m and QQQ -2.75% 1m, both below MA50 and MA200, while IWM remains above MA200; leadership is narrow rather than broad.
Current portfolio still fits the tape: Energy (XOM, CVX) and select quality compounders with stronger relative momentum (KLAC, TSM, GOOGL) continue to outrun weak index trend support.
No-go for rebalance today: cadence not due, edge from incremental turnover is low, and qualitative refresh did not show clear thesis damage severe enough to override the weekly spacing rule.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Momentum remains soft versus current leaders (1m 0.10%, 3m -10.21%, below MA50/MA200) and valuation is rich for decelerating growth: operating margin 41.8%, ROIC 39.9%, revenue growth 6.9%, EPS growth 7.6%, P/E 62.9.

Still high quality, but tape remains weak versus benchmark trend backdrop: 1m -2.38%, 3m -6.28%, below MA50/MA200. No verified 7-day headline refresh available; thesis damage not confirmed, but near-term momentum is less resilient than KLAC/XOM/CVX.

No thesis break, but monitor fundamental rollover beneath price strength: revenue growth -0.3% and EPS growth -2.7% TTM despite strong momentum. If commodity leadership fades, factor support could unwind quickly.

thesis: Catalyst: existing winners remain aligned with narrow market leadership, especially XOM/CVX/KLAC, while no new verified qualitative shock forces action inside the 30-day horizon.
Factor edge: preserving the current book avoids unnecessary turnover in a fragile tape where benchmarks remain below major trend levels and the portfolio already tilts toward relative strength.
Variant view: instead of rotating into fresh names, retain the prior mix until either breadth improves or a current holding shows explicit thesis damage via earnings/guidance/news.
risks: Macro: a sudden risk-on pivot or rates shock could punish the current defensive-energy/narrow-leadership tilt and favor lagging mega-cap growth instead.
Idiosyncratic: semis remain volatile; KLAC and TSM carry elevated vol/drawdown risk, and Energy holdings remain exposed to commodity reversals.
Execution/liquidity: low implementation risk in these large caps, but doing nothing risks opportunity cost if leadership rotates quickly before the next checkpoint.
falsifiable_checks: At next weekly checkpoint, if SPY and QQQ remain below MA200 and XOM/CVX stay positive on 30d returns, keep Energy overweight bias intact; if either energy name turns <=0% on 30d with breadth improving, reassess.
If AVGO or NVDA remain below both MA50 and MA200 next checkpoint while KLAC stays above both, treat relative semiconductor divergence as confirmed and consider a rebalance when due.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: not a rebalance day, and the current portfolio still matches a fragile, narrow-leadership regime, so expected alpha from trading does not clearly exceed turnover drag. Bull: existing leaders continue to compound and the unchanged book benefits from trend persistence. Bear: rotation broadens abruptly and some lagging holdings miss the turn, but the cost of waiting one scheduled interval is acceptable given no severe verified thesis break.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights—KLAC/XOM/CVX remain strongest momentum sleeves; GOOGL quality remains strong; AVGO and NVDA are watchlist laggards on trend. Risk budget—IT 50%, Energy 29%, Comm Services 14%, Industrials 7%; max single name 18%; crypto 0%. Scenario stress—equity index shock hurts NVDA/AVGO/TSM/KLAC most, commodity shock hits XOM/CVX, rates shock mixed, USD shock mainly ADR sensitivity in TSM; no single sector exceeds 80%. Execution plan—no trade, preserve weekly turnover discipline. Monitoring/kill-switch—revisit on next due rebalance or earlier only if severe thesis damage emerges from earnings/guidance/news; key triggers are loss of 30d momentum in Energy leaders, further breakdown below MA200 in semis, or materially negative company-specific updates. Qual refresh limits: verified fundamentals available for NVDA/AVGO/GOOGL/XOM; recent headline refresh UNKNOWN.
